title: Asukas Selection as Pilot
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's Selection as Pilot.             Quote Post by Ninkie » Tue Mar 19, 2013 10:43 pm I've looked for this via searching the forums, but never quite found what I'm looking for. Don't get me wrong. Asuka is, as far as I'm concerned, the most capable of the three pilots from the original series. But that judgement is based on skill... Shinji....has a bit of a cheat. Which leads me.... Who, considering Asuka's childhood and past, and considering SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Mar 19, 2013 10:55 pm The short answer is that her mother was already involved at some advanced level with the Evangelion project.

[]:             Quote Post by Ninkie » Tue Mar 19, 2013 11:03 pm Well I mean, I understand that. But so were many, many other people, as was suggested by SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Tue Mar 19, 2013 11:22 pm i don't think there's any indication of WHEN the students in the class all became eligible pilots. as you pointed out, Asuka was selected as a pilot very early on, meaning her mother's soul became part of an evangelion early on. perhaps the students in the class became eligible to be pilots much later than Asuka, so they had more time to focus on training Asuka to be skilled at piloting unit 02 which was noted to be the first eva specialized for combat. because it was specialized for combat, her piloting skills would matter more than Shinji's or Rei's.

[]:             Quote Post by Ninkie » Tue Mar 19, 2013 11:33 pm pioneer wrote: i don't think there's any indication of WHEN the students in the class all became eligible pilots. as you pointed out, Asuka was selected as a pilot very early on, meaning her mother's soul became part of an evangelion early on. perhaps the students in the class became eligible to be pilots much later than Asuka, so they had more time to focus on training Asuka to be skilled at piloting unit 02 which was noted to be the first eva specialized for combat. because it was specialized for combat, her piloting skills would matter more than Shinji's or Rei's.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 20, 2013 4:01 pm Like Yui, Asuka's mother worked directly with Evangelion's development and performed the contact experiment herself (in Kyoko's case, after Yui's "accident"). This was all happening at the very beginning of research and development. Once the CE was performed and a soul in the core of the new Eva, Asuka suddenly was chosen to be an Eva pilot. At this point, there was no Nerv, everything was still Gehirn, and there was probably no need for a Marduk Institute of a contingency of spare pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Wed Mar 20, 2013 9:13 pm My understanding is that the treaties governing the allotment of the Eva units give only a certain amount to any one nation at any given time. Technically speaking, Nerv Japan was borrowing Asuka/Unit-02 from Germany. They didn't have the headcount, so to speak, for one of the kids in the classroom (until it opened up later on, evidently, perhaps having something to do with the decimation of one of the American sites). Also: because hawt intense redheads are hard to find in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 20, 2013 9:37 pm user-02 wrote: My understanding is that the treaties governing the allotment of the Eva units give only a certain amount to any one nation at any given time.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 20, 2013 10:01 pm ...and it's nothing but an idiot ball excuse to put Asuka in 03 anyway, then promptly forgotten.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Mar 21, 2013 5:53 pm I've forgotten; was Kyoko's contact experiment mentioned in the show, or was it something we had to piece together later? Maybe it was mentioned in Death or something?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 21, 2013 6:06 pm IIRC, it was only made explicit in the NGE2 CI material; before that we only had the implication from Asuka's revelation in 25'

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Mar 21, 2013 7:57 pm Kyoko's Contact Experiment was mentioned in Episode 22: ATTENDEE A (OFF):“The hypothetical became reality. Talk about unlucky, the proponent becoming the test subject herself.” ATTENDEE B (OFF):“Then, that contact experiment was the direct cause?” ATTENDEE A (OFF):“Mental breakdown, that was the result of the contact.” ATTENDEE B (OFF):“But how cruel is it to commit suicide, leaving such a young daughter behind?” ATTENDEE C (OFF):“No, there might actually be more to this than that.”

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Mar 23, 2013 7:11 pm Yeah, given Kyoko's role in Unit 02's activation Asuka was the only possible choice for pilot. There was no reason to it beyond that.
